name: Paul Anka
abstract: Paul Albert Anka (born July 30, 1941) is a Canadian and American singer, songwriter, and actor. His songs include "Diana", "You Are My Destiny", "Lonely Boy", "Put Your Head on My Shoulder", "(You're) Having My Baby" and "My Way".

about_quotes.speaker: David Albert Francis
about_quotes.evidence: I did 'Lonely Boy' with Paul Anka and 'You Are My Destiny' with Paul Anka, and 'Patricia,' which we spoke about earlier

about_quotes.speaker: James Moody
about_quotes.evidence: I did Paul Anka. I did 5, 6 doubles. I had -- I had -- I had tenor, alto, baritone, bass clarinet, clarinet, flute -- no, 7 doubles ­ - and piccolo.
about_quotes.doc_title: James Moody — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bucky Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Just that little lick that you were singing reminds me of a Paul Anka song. ... [INTERVIEWEE] Oh yeah, we made a lot of those.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bucky Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: whether it was Paul Anka going [Shaw sings] “And you-uu and I-ii.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
